name: Lima
summary: Lima is the giant capital of Peru, sitting on cliffs above the Pacific Ocean. Its old town has golden Spanish churches, and Callao right next door is the busy port where ships sail in and out.
funFacts: Lima was founded by the Spanish in 1535 and was nicknamed the 'City of Kings' because rich rulers and ships full of gold passed through it.
An ancient mud-brick pyramid called Huaca Pucllana sits right in the middle of the city, built about 1,500 years ago by the Lima people.
Peru is the home of the potato — there are more than 4,000 different kinds, in colours like purple, blue, pink and bright yellow.
Brave paragliders launch off the Miraflores cliffs and float for ages above the Pacific Ocean and the busy beach road below.
Lima almost never gets proper rain, so people just wait out the grey 'donkey-belly' winter sky and call it a desert by the sea.
thingsToDo: Wander around Plaza Mayor in the old town to see the cathedral, the yellow government palace and the changing of the guard.
Go inside the San Francisco Monastery and tiptoe through the spooky underground catacombs full of old bones.
Visit the Larco Museum, a white colonial mansion packed with gold crowns, masks and pottery from ancient Peru.
Climb around Huaca Pucllana, the ancient mud-brick pyramid right in Miraflores, and meet the llamas that live there.
Stroll the Miraflores clifftop park and watch paragliders swooping above the Pacific Ocean far below.
Tour the Real Felipe fortress in Callao port, with thick stone walls, cannons and tall watchtowers facing the sea.

wildlife.name: Llama
wildlife.description: Friendly fluffy cousin of the camel that hums softly and carries packs over the Andes mountains.

wildlife.name: Humboldt Penguin
wildlife.description: Black-and-white seabird that dives in the cold Pacific currents off Peru's rocky coast.

wildlife.name: Andean Condor
wildlife.description: One of the world's biggest flying birds, with huge black wings spanning over three metres.

wildlife.name: Peruvian Pelican
wildlife.description: Big grey-and-white seabird with a long stretchy beak it uses to scoop up fish.

localWord.meaning: Hello / how are you? in Quechua, the language of the Incas.

images.alt: Wide view of Lima's Plaza Mayor with the cathedral and government palace around the main square.
The twin-towered yellow stone facade of Lima Cathedral on the Plaza de Armas.
The bright yellow stone Convent of San Francisco with its twin bell towers in old Lima.
The whitewashed colonial mansion that houses the Larco Museum in Lima.
The ancient stepped adobe pyramid of Huaca Pucllana surrounded by Lima's modern buildings.
Stone walls and watchtowers of the Real Felipe fortress in the port of Callao.
A fluffy brown-and-white llama standing in the Andes mountains.
A black-and-white Humboldt penguin standing on a rock.
A huge black Andean condor soaring above Colca Canyon in Peru.
A Peruvian pelican with a long beak resting on a post in Paracas, Peru.
